title: GOV.UK Upcoming Components and Patterns
summary: GOV.UK's current public list of components and patterns under consideration, showing how community needs, examples, use cases, code, and research findings inform prioritisation alongside maintenance of essential system components.
lessons: Publish upcoming work and the evidence still needed instead of presenting proposals as finished guidance.
Invite concrete designs, code, use cases, and research findings through a visible contribution path.
Balance community requests with maintenance of essential components and patterns.
caveats: GOV.UK's governance process reflects UK government service standards; adapt the transparency and evidence framework without importing government-specific process requirements.
licenseNote: OGL v3.0 for GOV.UK content; MIT for code.
antiImitationNote: Transfer transparent prioritisation and evidence-seeking practices; do not import UK government procurement or service-standard processes.
